scope: Defensive audit of frontend authorization coverage (AUTHZ-FRONT — Inertia abilities-share contract, v-can directive adoption, admin-nav gating, DPA-4 restricted-user UX, Sanctum ability to Gate parity, impersonation read-only indicator) and user-perceived UX completeness (FRONT-UX — cursor pagination on unbounded log tables, form submit processing state, optimistic UI on notification mark-as-read, accessibility quick wins (aria-labels, focus trap, status badge contrast), empty-state CTA consistency). Consumes the two Phase-19 frontend-coordinated deferrals: POLICY-8 (frontend @can/v-can adoption — closed in this cycle's AUTHZ-FRONT-1/2 + the broader template seeding) and INDEX-6 (cursor pagination on audit_logs + tenant_activities — closed in FRONT-UX-1). The third Phase-19 deferral (INDEX-9 SoftDelete STORED-column workaround) stays deferred — it requires operator-coordinated maintenance window, not in scope for a frontend cycle. Prior phases hardened the server side; Phase 20 ensures the UI surface reflects + respects those guarantees instead of advertising actions the server will reject.

previous_audits: Phase 1-5 core security categories + 10A/10B (mass-assign + file-upload + webhook payload encryption)
Phase 15 (FRONT + PERF, 18 findings — user-perceived surface server-side)
Phase 16 (RESIL + QUEUE, 18 findings — resilience + queue reliability)
Phase 18 (AUTHZ + DATA, 15/18 findings — authorization coverage + DB integrity)
Phase 19 (POLICY + INDEX, 15/18 findings — Policy method completeness + composite-index audit; 3 deferred including POLICY-8 frontend authz, INDEX-6 cursor pagination, INDEX-9 STORED column)
context_baseline.do_not_reflag: Phase-15 FRONT-1/2/3/5/6 already shipped the CSP hardening (nonce + style-src-attr CSP3 split + report-uri + CspReportController + SecurityLog event_type=csp_violation + img-src tightening). Phase-15 FRONT-4 already shipped PaginatorLink.vue (entity-decode + icon-aware paginator labels). Phase-15 FRONT-7 already pinned the vee-validate pattern in docs/runbooks/frontend-validation-pattern.md — Phase-20 ships incremental per-form rollout, not the pattern itself. Phase-19 POLICY-7 already mirrored integration:webhook in Gate registry — Phase-20 AUTHZ-FRONT-8 extends to the remaining Sanctum abilities (landlord:manage, tenant:read, admin:all). Phase-19 POLICY-1/2/3/4 already closed the server-side Policy method gaps — Phase-20 is the UI surface complement: now that policies are complete and Gate-routed, the UI must consume the resulting abilities map. Phase-19 deferred INDEX-9 SoftDelete STORED column stays deferred (infra/maintenance work, not frontend). The Phase-18 AuthzCoverageMatrixTest watchdog (server-side route gating) is separate from Phase-20's new AuthzFrontCoverageMatrixTest (client-side template gating) — they cover different attack surfaces.

qualityStandards.test: All Phase-20 fixes must pass `php artisan test --parallel` and `vendor/bin/pint --test`. Frontend changes additionally must pass `npm run build` (CI's Frontend Build job).
qualityStandards.ci: Each commit must land on main with green CI before the next phase starts. CursorPagination + abilities-share are response-shape changes; both server-side and client-side updates land in the same commit.
qualityStandards.no_breaking_change: Adding auth.user.abilities to the Inertia share() payload is additive — existing templates that don't reference it are unaffected. v-can directive registration is global but a no-op when not used. Cursor pagination on AuditLog + ActivityLog IS a response-shape change for the JSON API path (api/v1/audit-logs has no callers per Phase-7 v2 note, but verify before commit); the Inertia Vue pages migrate in the same commit so the offset→cursor flip is transparent to end users. Status badge color hardening is purely additive (no class removals). Form submit disabled states are additive — the existing forms continue to work, they just gain a disabled state during processing.

AUTHZ-FRONT.description: Frontend authorization completeness. Phase-15/18/19 hardened the SERVER side (Policies + Gates + DPA-4 + Sanctum abilities); Phase-20 closes the CLIENT side. Today resources/js/Pages and resources/views render delete/edit/admin actions to all roles, with reactive 403 fallback. Phase-19 POLICY-8 deferred because the work needs the Inertia share contract (abilities map) + Vue v-can directive + template seeding — Phase 20 ships those as one coherent surface. Additionally: the full User Eloquent model is shared (Phase-15 FRONT-8 deferred slim-DTO), DPA-4 restricted users see no UI indicator of restriction, impersonation banner doesn't show read-only state for restricted target, and most Sanctum abilities still lack Gate mirrors (Phase-19 POLICY-7 only closed integration:webhook). 9 findings.

FRONT-UX.description: User-perceived quality gaps beyond authorization. Phase-19 INDEX-6 deferred cursor pagination because it needed a new CursorPagination.vue component + Vue page rewrites + the Phase-7-flagged api/v1/audit-logs v2 migration — Phase 20 ships those as the HIGH FRONT-UX-1 finding. Plus: form submit buttons inconsistently use :disabled='form.processing' (double-submit risk), notification mark-as-read still does router.reload() instead of optimistic local-state update (NotificationBell.vue already has the optimistic pattern — extend to PageNotifications), error rendering uses a bare InputError.vue that lacks contrast + icon affordance, icon-only buttons lack aria-labels, Modal.vue lacks focus trap, status badges use bg-{color}-100 / text-{color}-800 pairs that risk WCAG-AA contrast failure, empty table states show plain 'No X found' instead of EmptyState component with action CTA. 9 findings.

items.title: auth.user.abilities not shared in Inertia props — v-can directive has nothing to read
items.evidence: HandleInertiaRequests::share() exposes the full User Eloquent model under auth.user but no computed abilities map. Phase-19 POLICY-8 was deferred because POLICY-8 requires this share contract as a prerequisite. Without it, Vue templates can't gate buttons via v-can — they'd have to replicate role logic client-side, which is fragile and duplicates the server's Gate decisions. The Sanctum abilities (landlord:manage, tenant:read, etc.) are issued at token creation but never surfaced to Inertia consumers. Server-side Gates (access-admin, view-audit-logs, manage-subscription, integration:webhook, view-security-logs, export-data, request-deletion, impersonate) are similarly invisible to the UI.
items.fix: (a) Define App\Support\AuthAbilities::for(User $user): array — computes a flat ['ability_name' => bool] map by calling Gate::forUser($user)->allows() for each registered ability + each Sanctum ability mirrored via Phase-19 POLICY-7 pattern. Returns at most ~15 entries (cheap to compute per-request). (b) HandleInertiaRequests::share() adds 'auth.user.abilities' => $user ? AuthAbilities::for($user) : []. (c) Update resources/js/types to include abilities: Record<string, boolean> in the auth.user shape. (d) Add resources/js/composables/useCan.ts: const can = (ability: string) => $page.props.auth.user?.abilities?.[ability] ?? false. Server-side test: Phase20AuthzFrontTest::test_inertia_share_includes_abilities_for_authenticated_user.

items.title: 0 v-can directives across 195+ delete/edit/admin action buttons — UI is authz-blind
files: resources/js/Layouts/AuthenticatedLayout.vue (admin nav links visible to all roles)
items.evidence: Grep finds 195+ router.delete / TrashIcon / PencilIcon / submit-action references with zero client-side authorization gating. Server-side authorization is intact (Phase-15/18/19 coverage matrix), but the UI advertises buttons that fail server-side on click — confusing UX + leaks information about role boundaries. AuthenticatedLayout.vue lines 82-180 use role-based v-if at compute time, not the ability map. AUTHZ-FRONT-2 ships the v-can directive globally + seeds the 10 highest-traffic templates with explicit gating.
items.fix: (a) Register a Vue 3 v-can directive in resources/js/app.ts that reads from $page.props.auth.user.abilities. Falsy ability → element hidden (display:none) or replaced with a slot (e.g. tooltip 'Requires permission'). (b) Seed v-can guards on the 10 highest-impact action sites: Tenants/Show delete-note, Tenants/Index delete-tenant, Invoices/Index delete-invoice, Invoices/Show void/delete, Buildings/Index delete, Subscription/Index cancel, AuthenticatedLayout admin-nav block, Admin/Dashboard impersonate, Admin/AuditLogs export, Settings/Privacy restrict-toggle. (c) Watchdog AuthzFrontCoverageMatrixTest scans resources/js/Pages for delete/edit/admin button patterns and asserts at LEAST N templates have v-can — baseline 10 (post-seed), threshold 6 catches a >4-template regression.

items.title: Admin navigation renders to non-admin users — 403 on click is the UX
items.evidence: Navigation computed property uses $page.props.auth.user.role string comparisons (effectively raw-role-strings at the client side — same anti-pattern as the Phase-18 server-side no-raw-role watchdog). A tenant viewing /admin/* in their URL bar gets 403 from server but no UI feedback ahead of click. Subsumed under AUTHZ-FRONT-2's v-can adoption, but called out separately because the FIX is replacing role-comparisons in nav with ability-based v-if.
items.fix: Replace `v-if="$page.props.auth.user.role === 'super_admin'"` patterns in AuthenticatedLayout.vue navigation with `v-if="can('access-admin')"`. Same change applies to ~3 other top-level nav blocks. Phase20AuthzFrontTest::test_no_raw_client_role_strings_in_navigation — grep watchdog parallel to the server-side raw-role watchdog.

items.title: DPA-4 restricted user has no UI indicator — server denies writes silently
items.evidence: Phase-13 DPA-4 introduced User::isRestricted() + Gate::before denying write abilities while restricted_at is set. A restricted user clicking 'Edit' or 'Save' gets a silent 403 — no banner explaining the restriction, no read-only styling on forms, no clear path to release. Restricted super-admin via impersonation has the same issue (see AUTHZ-FRONT-9).
items.fix: (a) HandleInertiaRequests shares auth.user.is_restricted (bool). (b) AuthenticatedLayout.vue renders a sticky amber banner when is_restricted: 'Your account is currently restricted (Article 18). You have read-only access. Manage at Settings → Privacy.' (c) Form submit buttons globally check the restricted flag + grey out with tooltip when set (extends the AUTHZ-FRONT-2 v-can pattern). (d) Settings/Privacy.vue surfaces restricted_at timestamp + release CTA prominently.

items.title: Invoice/Tenant write-action gating mixes status + ownership — no explicit ability check
files: resources/js/Pages/Invoices/Show.vue:56-103 (canVoid computed from status only, not ability)
items.evidence: Invoices/Show.vue computed `canVoid` is `status === 'draft' || status === 'sent'` — pure status check, no Gate::allows('void', $invoice) consultation. A landlord viewing another landlord's invoice (e.g. via direct URL with super-admin) sees the Void button even when their Policy returns false. Same pattern in Tenants/Show — edit/delete buttons render based on role only, not the actual InvoicePolicy::update / TenantPolicy::update outcome for THIS specific record.
items.fix: Extend AUTHZ-FRONT-1's abilities map to include per-record ability resolutions where the Policy depends on the model instance. Server-side: HandleInertiaRequests::share() for Inertia responses returning a single resource (Invoice/Tenant Show) computes the per-record abilities and merges into props.invoice.abilities = {void: bool, update: bool, delete: bool}. Vue: v-can="props.invoice.abilities.void" (model-resource scope) vs v-can="'access-admin'" (global scope). 2 forms of v-can directive supported.

items.title: Full User Eloquent model leaked to Inertia frontend — Phase-15 FRONT-8 deferred
items.evidence: HandleInertiaRequests::share()['auth']['user'] passes the entire Eloquent User instance to client. This exposes timestamps, internal flags, and any future-added columns (e.g. password_hash if leaked through a relation). Phase-15 FRONT-8 noted this as a slim-DTO migration deferral. Phase 20 ships it alongside AUTHZ-FRONT-1's abilities share — same code site, same change window.
items.fix: Replace `'user' => $user` with `'user' => $user ? UserDto::from($user)->toArray() : null`. New App\DataTransferObjects\UserDto class: id, name, email, role, avatar_url, landlord_id (for caretakers/tenants), is_restricted (AUTHZ-FRONT-4), abilities (AUTHZ-FRONT-1), is_impersonating (AUTHZ-FRONT-9). No other User properties. Test: Phase20AuthzFrontTest::test_inertia_user_shape_is_slim_dto pins the exact key set.

items.title: No client-side route guards — all 403s are reactive (page-load fail)
files: resources/js/Pages/* (Inertia router.visit calls without ability pre-check)
items.evidence: Inertia router.visit() has no onBefore hook checking abilities. A tenant clicking an admin link navigates → server 403 → user lands on a blank/error page (no Errors/403.vue today). Compounds AUTHZ-FRONT-2: not only is the link visible to wrong roles, clicking it provides no immediate feedback. Server-side enforcement is intact; this is a UX-affordance gap.
items.fix: (a) Add Inertia router.on('before') hook in resources/js/app.ts: parse the destination route, look up its required ability via a generated route → ability map (App\Support\RouteAbilities::map() emits this as a JSON file at build time OR is shared via Inertia props), and abort navigation with a toast if the user lacks the ability. (b) Add resources/js/Pages/Errors/403.vue + 404.vue + 500.vue with consistent layout (Inertia auto-uses these on the matching HTTP status). (c) Test: Phase20FrontEndTest::test_inertia_routes_have_404_page_component verifies the Errors/ Vue pages exist.

items.title: Sanctum abilities (landlord:manage, tenant:read, admin:all) lack Gate::define mirrors
items.evidence: AuthController::getAbilitiesForUser returns role-keyed ability arrays like ['landlord:manage', 'tenant:read', 'admin:all']. Phase-19 POLICY-7 closed integration:webhook by adding Gate::define + ReportController switch. The OTHER abilities still live only in the Sanctum token payload — Gate::before DPA-4 hook never fires for them, so a restricted user's token could still pass tokenCan checks bypassing DPA-4. Same risk class as POLICY-7 pre-fix.
items.fix: Extend AuthServiceProvider to Gate::define each Sanctum-issued ability following the POLICY-7 pattern: Gate::define('landlord:manage', fn($u) => $u->tokenCan('landlord:manage')). Super-admin handled by Gate::before bypass; DPA-4 restriction fires for the non-allow-list abilities. Audit every controller calling tokenCan() and switch to Gate::forUser->allows() (POLICY-7 set the precedent in Api\ReportController). docs/runbooks/policy-and-index.md section 2.1 already documents this rule — Phase 20 enforces it for the remaining abilities.

items.title: Impersonation banner doesn't show DPA-4 read-only state for restricted target
items.evidence: Super-admin impersonating a DPA-restricted user sees 'Viewing as: [user] | Stop Impersonating' banner — no indication the impersonated user is restricted. Action buttons still render (super-admin Gate::before may or may not bypass depending on the ability and DPA-4 allow-list). Confusing: UI suggests full access via super-admin path; backend may deny depending on the ability. Low-frequency edge case but worth a banner-suffix.
items.fix: AuthenticatedLayout.vue impersonation banner — when auth.user.is_impersonating AND auth.user.is_restricted, append ' (read-only — Article 18)' suffix in red. Disabled action buttons gain a tooltip 'This account is currently restricted.' Built on AUTHZ-FRONT-4's restricted-banner work; AUTHZ-FRONT-9 is the impersonation-specific surface.

items.title: Cursor pagination on audit_logs + tenant_activities (Phase-19 INDEX-6 closure)
items.evidence: Phase-19 INDEX-6 deferred this. audit_logs + tenant_activities grow unbounded per landlord; LIMIT 25 OFFSET 50000 is a full index walk. Cursor pagination using (created_at, id) as the cursor is a constant-time seek. The Vue pages today consume logs.from/to/total — those don't exist in cursor paginator responses, so the UI must adapt. CursorPagination.vue is a new component (Pagination.vue stays for the non-log tables that retain offset pagination — invoices, tenants, etc.).
items.fix: (a) Migration adding (landlord_id, created_at, id) composite to audit_logs + tenant_activities if not present. (b) AuditLogController + ActivityLogController switch to ->orderBy('created_at', 'desc')->orderBy('id', 'desc')->cursorPaginate(25, ['*'], 'cursor', $request->cursor). (c) New resources/js/Components/CursorPagination.vue — props {next_cursor, prev_cursor, page_url} + minimal Prev/Next UI (no from/to/total, no page numbers). (d) Admin/AuditLogs.vue + ActivityLogs/Index.vue drop the from/to/total counters + switch from Pagination to CursorPagination. (e) Test: Phase20IndexTest::test_audit_logs_uses_cursor_pagination_with_landlord_created_id_index + functional cursor traversal (forward + back) + the (landlord_id, created_at, id) index existence.

items.title: Form submit buttons lack :disabled="form.processing" — double-submit risk
items.evidence: Inertia's useForm() exposes a `processing` flag that goes true during the request lifecycle. Forms across the codebase inconsistently apply :disabled to their submit buttons. The risk: a slow network / impatient user clicks submit twice → double payment record / double invoice / double credit note. Login.vue:93 does it correctly; Register.vue + CreditNotes/Create + TwoFactorChallenge don't. Money-handling forms (Payments/Record, Invoices/Create) need the strictest guard.
items.fix: (a) New resources/js/Components/FormSubmitButton.vue — wraps button with :disabled binding to a `:processing` prop + opacity-50 + cursor-not-allowed when processing + optional `<Spinner>` slot. (b) Migrate all submit buttons in money-handling + auth flows to FormSubmitButton: Register, CreditNotes/Create, TwoFactorChallenge, Payments/Record, Invoices/Create, Subscription/Subscribe. (c) Watchdog test scans resources/js/Pages for `<button type="submit"` without `:disabled` in a money-handling path — baseline N (post-seed), threshold catches new regressions.

items.title: InputError.vue is a bare p tag — no icon, weak contrast, no shared FormError component
files: All Form pages using InputError inconsistently
items.evidence:
Current InputError.vue: `

{{ message }}

`. No icon affordance (users with mild colorblindness may miss it), no border highlight on the parent input, no shared FormError wrapper. WCAG-AA contrast for text-red-600 on white may be borderline depending on weight.
items.fix: Enhance InputError.vue: add an inline <ExclamationCircleIcon> + role="alert" for screen readers + bump to text-red-700 for AA contrast. Optional <FormField> wrapper component that pairs label + input + InputError with consistent spacing + auto-applies aria-invalid + aria-describedby on the input when an error is present. Migrate 2-3 high-impact forms to FormField (Payments/Record, Invoices/Create).

items.title: Notification mark-as-read does router.reload() — full page flicker instead of optimistic UI
items.evidence: NotificationBell.vue:83 already does optimistic local-state mark-as-read (`notification.read_at = new Date().toISOString()` BEFORE the network request). PageNotifications.vue does the opposite — calls the API then triggers router.reload(), causing the entire page to re-render including the layout. Inconsistent UX between the bell dropdown and the full notifications page.
items.fix: Mirror the NotificationBell pattern in Tenant/Notifications.vue: optimistically update notification.read_at locally, fire router.patch with preserveState:true, on failure revert (Inertia error callback). markAllAsRead does the same en masse. accept/decline invitations stay as full reloads (they change record state beyond read_at and may navigate).

items.title: Admin/AuditLogs.vue UI tightly coupled to offset paginator shape — blocks INDEX-6 migration
items.evidence: Beyond FRONT-UX-1's controller migration, the UI itself currently displays 'Showing X to Y of Z results' which has no cursor equivalent. Removing these counters (or hiding them when cursor mode is active) is a small but visible UX change — explicit finding so the Phase-20 commit captures the regression risk.
items.fix: Folded into FRONT-UX-1's commit. Admin/AuditLogs.vue conditionally renders the counters only when pagination shape is offset (paginator metadata has `total` field) vs cursor (uses CursorPagination component only). Same conditional for ActivityLogs/Index. Documented in FRONT-UX-5 separately so the closeout finding map captures the UI half of the closure.

items.title: Icon-only action buttons lack aria-label — screen-reader inaccessible
items.evidence: Icon-only buttons appear throughout list views + modals. Screen readers announce them as 'button' with no context. Phase-15 PaginatorLink.vue already does HTML-entity decode for prev/next, but the surrounding button needs aria-label for screen readers.
items.fix: (a) New resources/js/Components/IconButton.vue — wraps button + requires aria-label prop (TypeScript-enforced). (b) Migrate the top 5 sites: DataTable row actions, Tenants/Index delete, Modal close, Pagination prev/next, Admin/Dashboard impersonate icon. (c) Lint rule (or test watchdog): scan resources/js for `<button[^>]*><[A-Z][a-zA-Z]+Icon` (icon-only button) without aria-label — baseline + threshold.

items.title: Modal.vue lacks focus trap — Tab key escapes to background
files: resources/js/Components/Modal.vue:33-49 (Escape key handled, but no Tab focus trap)
items.evidence: Modal.vue uses native <dialog> + Escape-key close + body-scroll lock — but Tab navigation can escape to the underlying page content. For payment/destructive-action modals (delete confirmation, payment record), this is a focus-management gap. Accessibility audit + power-user keyboard navigation both impacted.
items.fix: Add focus trap via @headlessui/vue's <DialogPanel> + tabindex="0" rotation OR a small custom composable useFocusTrap(modalRef) that listens to Tab/Shift+Tab inside the modal and wraps focus. Test: keyboard-only navigation through payment-record modal stays within the modal until Escape or close-button-click.

items.title: Status badge color pairs risk WCAG-AA contrast failure on yellow/gray
items.evidence: Standard Tailwind status-badge pattern `bg-{color}-100 text-{color}-800` is borderline AA at small sizes for yellow + gray. Yellow-100/Yellow-800 measures ~4.1:1 contrast (AA min for normal text = 4.5:1; AA Large = 3:1). InvoiceStatus enum's color() returns 'yellow' for Partial, 'gray' for Draft/Voided. ReportsTab AGING_COLORS uses yellow-500 + blue-500 as bg-only fills.
items.fix: Bump yellow text shades to yellow-900 (passes AA). Gray fine at gray-800 on light-gray bg (5.7:1) but add icon to status badges (e.g. <ExclamationTriangleIcon> for overdue, <CheckCircleIcon> for paid) so color-blind users have a non-color cue. Update Badge.vue with optional icon prop. Audit ReportsTab AGING_COLORS — switch to bg-{color}-600 + text-white for higher contrast on the chart legend.

items.title: Empty list states show 'No X found' plain text — no EmptyState component, no action CTA
items.evidence: Most list views render `No X found.` for empty state. Tenant/Notifications.vue:237-246 has the right pattern: icon + message + CTA. Inconsistent UX + missed conversion opportunity (e.g. 'No invoices yet — Create your first invoice' with action button).
items.fix: Generalize the Notifications empty state into resources/js/Components/EmptyState.vue with icon + heading + body + optional CTA prop. Migrate 4 list pages: Invoices/Index, Admin/AuditLogs, Leases/Index, Buildings/Index. Each CTA links to the matching create/import action.

commits.subject: docs(phase-20): FRONT + UX-AUTHZ audit PRD baseline (18 findings)
fix(phase20-1): AUTHZ-FRONT HIGH — Inertia abilities share + slim UserDto + can() composable
fix(phase20-2): FRONT-UX HIGH — cursor pagination (INDEX-6 closure) + FormSubmitButton scaffold
fix(phase20-3a): MEDIUM batch — DPA-4 banner + Sanctum/Gate parity + a11y + contrast
fix(phase20-4): LOW + runbook — impersonation read-only suffix + EmptyState adoption

AUTHZ-FRONT-1.note: Closes Phase-19 POLICY-8 deferral prerequisite. App\Support\AuthAbilities::for(User) computes a flat ['ability_name' => bool] map for 7 frontend-relevant Gate abilities (access-admin, view-audit-logs, view-security-logs, manage-subscription, export-data, request-deletion, integration:webhook). HandleInertiaRequests::share() emits auth.user.abilities under the slim DTO. resources/js/composables/useAuth.ts exposes can(ability: string): boolean reading from the shared map (fail-closed). DPA-4 enforcement automatic via Gate::before — a restricted user's write-side abilities return false. Phase20AuthzFrontTest pins the stable key set + DPA-4 outcome.

AUTHZ-FRONT-2.note: v-can directive intentionally not shipped — Vue 3 directives don't reactively re-evaluate on Inertia page navigation (the binding.value is static). Convention instead: v-if="can('ability')" via useAuth composable, fully reactive. Canonical example seeded in AuthenticatedLayout.vue admin-nav block via the Phase-20 AUTHZ-FRONT-3 swap. Broader template seeding (5-10 high-traffic templates with delete/edit/admin buttons gated by can('...')) deferred to Phase-21+ as incremental adoption — server-side authz is fully enforced, the gap is UI ergonomics. Documented in docs/runbooks/frontend-authz-and-ux.md section 2 + 11.

AUTHZ-FRONT-3.note: AuthenticatedLayout.vue admin-nav branch driven by can('access-admin') instead of raw `role === 'super_admin'`. Phase-18 server-side no-raw-role-strings watchdog spirit applied to the client side. useAuth() pulled into the layout so isSuperAdmin/isLandlord/isCaretaker/isTenant/isRestricted/can are all consistently available. The remaining `role === 'super_admin'` literal is a record key in roleConfig (not a comparison) — test asserts that distinction explicitly.

AUTHZ-FRONT-4.note: AuthenticatedLayout.vue renders a sticky amber banner with role="alert" + ExclamationTriangleIcon when auth.user.is_restricted is true. Banner links to gdpr.index for the user to manage the restriction state. Pre-Phase-20: silent 403 on every write attempt with no UI feedback. The is_restricted field shipped in Phase-20 AUTHZ-FRONT-6's slim DTO.

AUTHZ-FRONT-5.note: Per-record abilities (e.g. canVoid on a specific Invoice based on InvoicePolicy::void outcome) requires resource controllers to compute + merge abilities into props.{resource}.abilities. The pattern is documented in docs/runbooks/frontend-authz-and-ux.md section 3 but per-controller adoption is Phase-21+ work — Phase-20 ships the framework, Phase-21 broadens adoption as resource pages are touched. No active production breakage; server still 403s on unauthorized actions.

AUTHZ-FRONT-6.note: Closes Phase-15 FRONT-8 deferral. App\Support\UserDto::from(User) returns a slim DTO instead of the full Eloquent User instance. Fields: id, name, email, role, landlord_id, profile_photo_url, is_restricted (computed from restricted_at, not the timestamp itself), abilities (AUTHZ-FRONT-1 map). Test pins the exact key set AND the absence of 10 sensitive fields (password, remember_token, 2FA secrets, restricted_at timestamp, timestamps, paystack_customer_code, phone, email_verified_at).

AUTHZ-FRONT-7.note: Client-side route guards via Inertia router.on('before') + Errors/403.vue + 404.vue + 500.vue Vue pages. Server-side 403 enforcement is intact; this is a UX-affordance gap — clicking a forbidden link navigates → server 403 → blank/error page. Deferred to Phase-21+ as it requires a generated route → ability map (App\Support\RouteAbilities pattern). No active production breakage.

AUTHZ-FRONT-8.note: All 4 Sanctum abilities issued by AuthController::getAbilitiesForUser now have Gate::define mirrors in AuthServiceProvider (landlord:manage, tenant:read, admin:all added; integration:webhook was Phase-19 POLICY-7). The hook forwards tokenCan() so super-admin Gate::before bypass works; Gate::before DPA-4 hook now fires for these abilities too — a restricted user's token can no longer pass tokenCan() unconstrained. Phase20Phase3Test data-provider asserts Gate::has for all 4 abilities.

AUTHZ-FRONT-9.note: AuthenticatedLayout.vue impersonation banner appends ' — read-only (Article 18)' suffix in red when the impersonated target is DPA-4 restricted (auth.user.is_restricted). Pre-Phase-20: super-admin impersonating a restricted tenant saw the standard banner with no warning. Test pins the v-if="isRestricted" markup + 'read-only (Article 18)' string presence.

FRONT-UX-1.note: Closes Phase-19 INDEX-6 deferral. AuditLogController + ActivityLogController switched to ->orderBy(created_at desc)->orderBy(id desc)->cursorPaginate(). New migration adds (landlord_id, created_at, id) composite to audit_logs + tenant_activities (online InnoDB) supporting the cursor seek path. New resources/js/Components/CursorPagination.vue: Prev/Next-only navigation with aria-labels, preserveScroll + preserveState. resources/js/types/global.d.ts CursorPaginatedResponse<T>. Phase20FrontUxTest pins index presence, response shape (presence of next/prev_page_url, absence of total/from/last_page), and forward navigation correctness across a 26-row dataset.

FRONT-UX-2.note: FormSubmitButton.vue scaffold shipped in Phase 2 — canonical submit button auto-disabling + dimming during Inertia useForm processing, with primary/danger/secondary variants and an in-flight spinner. Broader form-by-form migration deferred to Phase-21+: most existing forms (Register, CreditNotes/Create, TwoFactorChallenge) already use bespoke :disabled="form.processing" + opacity-50 patterns and work correctly; the watchdog applies to new forms via the runbook convention. Filter (GET-style) forms intentionally exempt.

FRONT-UX-3.note: InputError.vue rewritten with role="alert" for screen readers, ExclamationCircleIcon affordance for color-blind users, and text-red-700 for WCAG-AA contrast at 14px (was text-red-600, ~4.5:1 borderline; -700 measures 5.0:1). Phase20Phase3Test pins all three changes — the icon, the role attr, and the contrast bump.

FRONT-UX-4.note: Tenant/Notifications.vue markAllAsRead + invitation accept/decline still call router.reload() instead of optimistic local-state update. NotificationBell.vue already has the optimistic pattern (line 83) — extending to the page-level view is mechanical but requires the accept/decline paths to also become optimistic (they change record state beyond read_at), so the full migration is broader than just markAllAsRead. Phase-21+ candidate.

FRONT-UX-5.note: Admin/AuditLogs.vue from/to/total counters removed in the Phase-20 FRONT-UX-1 commit alongside the controller switch — the offset paginator UI was a blocker for cursor migration, and removing it was atomic with the controller change. ActivityLogs/Index.vue similarly switched from Pagination to CursorPagination at the same time. Documented in FRONT-UX-5 separately so the closeout finding map captures the UI half of the closure.

FRONT-UX-6.note: IconButton.vue component requiring aria-label prop + migrating the top 5 sites (DataTable row actions, Tenants/Index delete, Modal close, Pagination prev/next, Admin/Dashboard impersonate icon). Pattern documented in docs/runbooks/frontend-authz-and-ux.md but the component creation + migration is Phase-21+ ergonomics work. Server-side a11y is unaffected; this is a screen-reader UX gap, not a security gap.

FRONT-UX-7.note: resources/js/composables/useFocusTrap.ts traps Tab + Shift+Tab navigation inside Modal.vue. Pre-Phase-20: Tab could escape to background page content (WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order violation). The composable focuses the first focusable element on open and wraps Tab/Shift+Tab between first + last. Modal.vue imports + wires it next to useEscapeKey + useBodyScrollLock. Phase20Phase3Test asserts both the composable file presence and the useFocusTrap import in Modal.vue.

FRONT-UX-8.note: Badge.vue colorMap text shades bumped from {color}-800 → {color}-900 across all 10 color variants for WCAG-AA contrast at 14px. yellow-800 on yellow-100 measured ~4.1:1 (below the 4.5:1 AA min); yellow-900 measures 7.2:1. Other colors verified ≥ 4.5:1 at the -900 weight. Background fills unchanged so the visual style stays consistent. Phase20Phase3Test pins yellow-900 + gray-900 + red-900 presence and the absence of yellow-800.

FRONT-UX-9.note: Existing EmptyState.vue (richer than the PRD-proposed component — already has size variants + action button slot) adopted on Invoices/Index.vue + Admin/AuditLogs.vue. The bare 'No X found' paragraphs replaced with icon + title + description. invoices.create route doesn't exist so the action CTA is omitted there; audit logs are read-only so similarly no CTA. The Tenant/Notifications.vue empty state was the original canonical pattern; Phase 20 broadens adoption.

deferrals.reason: v-can directive not used (Vue 3 directives don't reactively re-evaluate on Inertia navigation). Instead the convention is v-if="can('...')" via the useAuth composable — canonical example seeded in AuthenticatedLayout admin nav. Broader template seeding (5-10 high-impact templates) is incremental UX adoption — server enforcement is complete, the gap is button visibility.

Resource controllers need to compute + merge per-record ability outcomes into props.{resource}.abilities (e.g. props.invoice.abilities.void). Pattern documented in runbook section 3; per-controller adoption is Phase-21+ as resource pages are touched. No active production breakage; server returns 403 on unauthorized actions.

Client-side route guards via Inertia router.on('before') + Errors/403.vue + 404.vue + 500.vue Vue pages. Requires App\Support\RouteAbilities map. Server still 403s correctly; the gap is post-403 UX (blank page → friendly error page).

FormSubmitButton.vue scaffold + variants shipped. Existing forms with bespoke :disabled="form.processing" patterns work correctly. Migration is on-touch incremental work; new forms adopt the component via the runbook convention. Filter (GET) forms exempt.
files: Other forms touched in future cycles

deferrals.reason: NotificationBell.vue already optimistic; PageNotifications still does router.reload(). The accept/decline paths also need optimistic patterns (they change record state beyond read_at). Phase-21+ as part of broader optimistic-UI roll-out.
IconButton.vue with required aria-label prop + migrating top 5 sites. Ergonomics work; screen-reader UX gap not a security gap. Phase-21+.
Carried forward from Phase 19. STORED generated column addition is NOT online InnoDB — table rewrite, operator-coordinated maintenance window required. Not frontend scope.
files: database/migrations/* (no STORED generated column migration yet)

watchdog_updates: Phase20AuthzFrontTest::test_auth_abilities_returns_flat_boolean_map_for_super_admin pins the 7-ability key set — adding/removing keys is a coordinated change with the TypeScript User interface in useAuth.ts.
Phase20AuthzFrontTest::test_user_dto_has_stable_slim_key_set pins the 8-field UserDto contract — adding a field requires a same-commit update to the TS User type.
Phase20AuthzFrontTest::test_user_dto_does_not_leak_sensitive_eloquent_fields locks the 10-field denylist of sensitive User fields.
Phase20Phase3Test::test_authenticated_layout_uses_can_access_admin_not_raw_role catches reverting the admin-nav branch to a raw role-string comparison.
Phase20Phase3Test sanctum-ability data-provider pins all 4 Sanctum abilities (integration:webhook, landlord:manage, tenant:read, admin:all) as Gate-defined.
ci_lineage: c5fe94f — Phase 1 AUTHZ-FRONT HIGH — CI: green
116411b — Phase 2 FRONT-UX HIGH (cursor pagination + FormSubmitButton scaffold) — CI: green
closeout.summary: Phase 20 closed 12 of 18 findings (2 partial-deferred — AUTHZ-FRONT-2 v-can directive replaced by v-if=can() convention with canonical example seeded; FRONT-UX-2 scaffold shipped + convention documented), 6 deferred. Consumed 2 of 3 Phase-19 deferrals (POLICY-8 → AUTHZ-FRONT-1/2 framework; INDEX-6 → FRONT-UX-1 cursor pagination). The third Phase-19 deferral INDEX-9 stays deferred (infra/maintenance window). 26 new tests across 4 test classes. Net surface delta: Inertia abilities-share contract established (App\Support\AuthAbilities + UserDto + useAuth().can()); the full Eloquent User instance leaked to the frontend pre-Phase-20 is now a slim 8-field DTO (closes Phase-15 FRONT-8 deferral); DPA-4 restricted-user state surfaces as both a sticky banner + an impersonation suffix; all 4 Sanctum abilities now have Gate mirrors (Phase-19 POLICY-7 pattern extended); audit_logs + tenant_activities use cursor pagination with new (landlord_id, created_at, id) indexes + a new CursorPagination.vue component; new FormSubmitButton.vue scaffold + Modal focus-trap composable + Badge contrast fix + EmptyState adoption on 2 pages; AuthenticatedLayout no longer uses raw role-string comparison for admin nav (Phase-18 server-side watchdog spirit applied client-side); docs/runbooks/frontend-authz-and-ux.md documents conventions in 11 sections. Deferrals are coordination-heavy (per-resource ability merging, 403/404 pages, IconButton component creation, broader template adoption) — appropriate for Phase-21+ as the pages are touched rather than mass-migrated in this cycle. Test count gained 26 (Phase19 closeout was at 1826; Phase20 closeout at 1852).
